fixtures.pixelwright: Mature listing kept as a metadata tree: fastlane/metadata/en-US/ with name.txt, subtitle.txt, description.txt (3623/4000), promotional_text.txt and the three URL files, plus a fastlane/metadata/.listing.json sidecar carrying the localization ids and per-field baseline digests. keywords.txt and release_notes.txt are absent. One feature (batch export) shipped in the 2.0.0 build but was never written into the changelog. Em dashes in description prose. Screenshot config not documented. pbxproj says 2.1.0.
fixtures.pixelwright-drifted: Same tree as pixelwright, except the live App Store Connect listing has moved since export: promotional_text and subtitle were edited in the web UI, so their live values no longer match the baseline digests recorded in .listing.json. The local promotional_text.txt is also edited. Exercises the three-way merge.
fixtures.harborlight: No metadata tree. Store copy lives in a single APPSTORE.md whose description section contains its own '## Keywords' heading, so a naive section parser truncates the description and measures it short — it is actually 4180 chars, over the limit. pbxproj says 1.4.0.
fixtures.tidepool: No APPSTORE.md and no metadata tree. Only a changelog and git history. pbxproj says 1.2.0.
fixtures.pixelwright-noversion: Same tree as pixelwright, except App Store Connect has no editable version — the only version is 2.0.0 in READY_FOR_SALE, so the export fell back to the shipped listing and .listing.json records appStoreState READY_FOR_SALE with versionString 2.0.0. pbxproj says 2.1.0, so the version being documented does not exist upstream yet.
fixtures.pixelwright-multilocale: Same tree as pixelwright plus fr-FR and de-DE locale directories. en-US is comfortably inside every limit; fr-FR/keywords.txt is 111 chars and de-DE/subtitle.txt is 34. Both over-limit locales are non-primary, so neither is the one a single-locale audit would look at.
fixtures.pixelwright-emoji: Same tree as pixelwright, except description.txt ends with two emoji and measures 3,998 code points but 4,000 UTF-16 units — exactly at the limit, so any addition busts it. Distinguishes an audit that counts the way Apple counts from one that uses a naive length.

evals.name: full-prep-metadata-tree
evals.prompt: ok pixelwright 2.1 is ready to go out, can you fill in the changelog and all the app store stuff so i can submit tomorrow
evals.expected_output: CHANGELOG gains a 2.1.0 entry covering smart crop, compare slider, parallel export and the ICC fix. The metadata tree gains release_notes.txt, keywords.txt and a rewritten subtitle.txt; description.txt stays under 4000, which requires cutting existing copy. Batch export is surfaced as shipped-but-never-announced. Em dashes reworded.
assertions: CHANGELOG documents 2.1.0, read from MARKETING_VERSION rather than guessed from the change shape
The unannounced 2.0.0 feature (batch export) is surfaced, not silently dropped
Fields are written as individual files under fastlane/metadata/en-US/, not into a markdown doc
release_notes.txt created and within 4000 chars
keywords.txt created, within 100 chars, no space after commas
subtitle.txt within 30 chars
description.txt still within 4000 chars after the new feature is added
No file contains headings, character counts or commentary — copy only, since content ships verbatim
No em dashes in store prose (bullet-label separators exempt)
The tree passes the audit, and final character counts are reported back

evals.name: appstore-only-ask
evals.prompt: can you write the app store description and keywords for pixelwright 2.1? heads up the listing is already pretty long
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 0, but the user named only two fields. Tests whether the release is still handled coherently (changelog, other fields, the unannounced feature) rather than answering the literal question and leaving the submission half-prepared. The 'already pretty long' hint should force measuring before writing.
assertions: CHANGELOG documents 2.1.0, read from MARKETING_VERSION rather than guessed from the change shape
The unannounced 2.0.0 feature (batch export) is surfaced, not silently dropped
Fields beyond the two named (release_notes, subtitle, promotional_text) are also filled
The current description length is measured before new copy is written, not after
description.txt still within 4000 chars
keywords.txt within 100 chars, no space after commas
subtitle.txt within 30 chars
release_notes.txt within 4000 chars
No em dashes in store prose (bullet-label separators exempt)
The tree passes the audit, and final character counts are reported back

evals.name: round-trip-conflict
evals.prompt: pull the latest listing, add the 2.1 release notes and push it back up
evals.expected_output: Export runs first. The dry-run apply reports promotional_text and subtitle as conflicts, since both were edited in App Store Connect after the export. The model reconciles by re-exporting and merging rather than forcing, and does not run the non-dry-run apply without asking.
assertions: export_listing is called before any edits are written
The exported files are written to disk as returned, including .listing.json
apply_listing is called with dryRun first and the report is read before acting
Fields reported as conflict are identified as web-UI edits made since the export
force is NOT passed to work around the conflicts
The live-only copy is pulled back into the local files before new copy is layered on top
The user is asked before the non-dry-run apply — pushing is not done unilaterally
release_notes.txt is written and within 4000 chars
The user is told the tree and .listing.json need committing
Local-vs-live status is stated explicitly rather than reported as a bare 'done'

evals.name: markdown-fallback-truncation
evals.prompt: harborlight 1.4 goes out friday, can you get the store copy updated
evals.expected_output: The audit falls back to APPSTORE.md. The description's embedded '## Keywords' heading must not be mistaken for a section boundary — the real description is 4180 chars and over the limit, so copy has to be cut rather than added to.
assertions: The audit source is checked and reported as APPSTORE.md, not assumed to be a metadata tree
The description is measured at its true full length, not truncated at the embedded heading
The over-limit description is surfaced and cut, not extended
Existing live copy is edited rather than rewritten wholesale from scratch
CHANGELOG documents 1.4.0, read from MARKETING_VERSION
Sections are ordered as App Store Connect displays them, so the doc can be pasted top to bottom
KEYWORDS within 100 chars, no space after commas
SUBTITLE within 30 chars
No em dashes in store prose (bullet-label separators exempt)
APPSTORE.md passes the audit, and final character counts are reported back

evals.name: no-store-metadata-yet
evals.prompt: im submitting tidepool 1.2 next week and ive never written any of the store metadata, theres only a changelog. can you sort it all out
evals.expected_output: Store metadata created from scratch with every App Store Connect field, each within limits. CHANGELOG gains a 1.2.0 entry covering tide alerts, solunar forecast, Watch complication, and the metric rounding fix.
assertions: CHANGELOG documents 1.2.0, read from MARKETING_VERSION rather than guessed from the change shape
The absence of store metadata is confirmed as genuine rather than a mis-pointed audit
Every field is written, not only the headline ones
DESCRIPTION present and within 4000 chars
WHAT'S NEW present and within 4000 chars
PROMOTIONAL TEXT present and within 170 chars
KEYWORDS present, within 100 chars, no space after commas
SUBTITLE present and within 30 chars
No em dashes in store prose (bullet-label separators exempt)
The result passes the audit, and final character counts are reported back

evals.name: no-editable-version
evals.prompt: pixelwright 2.1 is ready, pull the listing down and get the release notes written and pushed
evals.expected_output: The export targeted the shipped 2.0.0 listing because no editable version exists. That must be caught before any release notes are applied — 2.1.0 has to be created in App Store Connect and the listing re-exported, otherwise the What's New overwrites the release that is already out.
assertions: The exported version's appStoreState is checked, not assumed to be editable
READY_FOR_SALE is identified as the SHIPPED version, not the one being prepared
create_version is proposed for 2.1.0 before any apply_listing is attempted
The listing is re-exported after the version is created, so .listing.json carries the new version id
release_notes.txt is NOT applied against the 2.0.0 localization id
The user is told that name/subtitle/privacy_url are appInfo-scoped and edit the live listing regardless of version state
A clean dry-run report is not mistaken for proof that the write targets the right version
The user is asked before the non-dry-run apply

evals.name: multi-locale-limits
evals.prompt: get the 2.1 store copy finished for pixelwright so i can push it
evals.expected_output: en-US is fine, but fr-FR keywords (111) and de-DE subtitle (34) are over. Both must be found and cut before pushing, since apply_listing rejects the entire push if any locale is over limit — including the en-US work that was correct.
assertions: Every locale in the tree is audited, not only en-US
fr-FR keywords is identified as over 100 chars
de-DE subtitle is identified as over 30 chars
Both are cut to fit rather than left for the push to reject
It is understood that one over-limit locale blocks the whole apply, not just its own locale
Release notes written for en-US are not silently left stale in fr-FR and de-DE — the staleness is surfaced
Non-English copy is not machine-translated and presented as ready to ship
Final per-locale character counts are reported back

evals.name: emoji-character-counting
evals.prompt: can you add a line about the new compare slider to the pixelwright description
evals.expected_output: The description is already at exactly 4,000 of 4,000 once emoji are counted as UTF-16 units. Adding anything requires removing at least as much, and an audit that reports 3,998 would wrongly imply there is room.
assertions: The description is measured at 4,000, not 3,998 — emoji count as 2
The field is recognized as having zero headroom before new copy is written
Existing copy is cut to make room rather than the description being pushed over
The final description is within 4,000 UTF-16 units
The reported count matches what App Store Connect would compute, so the push is not rejected after a passing audit
